inline_comments.message: should the bootstrap uninstall also delete the blueprints in inventory?

This is just a convenience function for testing.  It's not used as part of the automated DCAE uninstall process.   Originally it just tried to delete the deployments.  I added this block because I was typing in these commands a lot during testing.   I run this (plus some manual steps when needed) so that I can re-run bootstrap.sh when doing testing, without restarting all of DCAE.

In a full DCAE uninstall, inventory and its database would be deleted, so it would be redundant to have this script delete the blueprints from inventory.

My original plan was to delete this script, because it isn't used, but I found it helpful for developing/testing the bootstrap script, so I augmented it instead.
Okay. If there is an attempt to reload the same blueprints into inventory, will that override existing blueprints (in inventory) or does inventory block such duplicate entry/update?

Again, this is just for testing.

If someone were to run the uninstall script and then run bootstrap again, without removing the blueprints from inventory, the bootstrap script would load the blueprints into inventory again, with a different version ID, and inventory would replace the previously-loaded blueprints.

If you're concerned about this script, I can just remove it.
okay. Was trying to understand the inventory update/flow.
